powershell-encoding.label: PowerShell encoding integrity
powershell-encoding.description: Preserve Korean and structured text across PowerShell, cmd, files, and child-process boundaries.

humanApprovalPoints: Review byte-level or round-trip evidence before accepting repaired text.
requiredCapabilities: Inspect encoding metadata and verify a UTF-8 round trip.

rules: Treat Windows PowerShell 5.1 defaults as ambiguous; specify UTF-8 explicitly for every file and process boundary.
Do not infer correctness from terminal appearance alone; verify saved bytes and decode the value again.
Prefer passing structured input through a UTF-8 file or stdin over shell interpolation.

root-cause-diagnosis.label: Direct-evidence root cause diagnosis
root-cause-diagnosis.description: Separate direct observations from proxy signals before changing code or policy.

humanApprovalPoints: Review the falsification evidence before declaring a root cause.
requiredCapabilities: Inspect direct process, authorization, quota, and exit-code evidence.

rules: Record the observed failure, competing hypotheses, and a falsifying check for each hypothesis.
Do not use names, prefixes, timestamps, or process existence as proof of actor identity, delivery, or success.
Prefer the smallest direct probe and retain its exit code and evidence.

path-escape-qa.label: Path containment QA
path-escape-qa.description: Detect traversal, encoded separator, and sibling-prefix escapes at filesystem boundaries.

requiredCapabilities: Normalize path inputs and compare separator-bounded resolved paths.

rules: Resolve both root and candidate before containment checks and require equality or a separator-bounded descendant.
Test dot segments, alternate separators, URL-encoded separators, absolute paths, and sibling-prefix collisions.
Fail closed when decoding or normalization is ambiguous.

balance-tuning.label: Deterministic balance tuning
balance-tuning.description: Tune parameters against explicit metrics without mutating baselines or hiding no-solution outcomes.

humanApprovalPoints: Approve target metrics and constraints before applying a candidate balance patch.
requiredCapabilities: Read a BalanceSpec and ObservationSet and reproduce the search deterministically.

rules: Define target metrics, companion continuous metrics, constraints, and search bounds before simulation.
Keep the product target separate from the search heuristic and preserve every observation.
Use a deterministic seed or explicit grid, report no-solution honestly, and never mutate the baseline during search.

instruction-contract-authoring.label: Instruction contract authoring
instruction-contract-authoring.description: Turn prose directives into internally consistent, adversarially testable contracts.

humanApprovalPoints: Approve policy-changing directives and their negative tests.
requiredCapabilities: Classify constraints and validate examples, gates, and generated hashes together.

rules: Classify each requirement as invariant, conditional rule, or preference before writing enforcement.
Add at least one adversarial negative case for every blocking gate.
Generate hashes and derived values from programs, and verify prose, schema, and harness agree.

promotion-review.label: Experience promotion review
promotion-review.description: Promote repeated experience into the narrowest durable artifact with evidence.

humanApprovalPoints: Approve promotion into a shared harness, skill, or wiki entry.
requiredCapabilities: Score repeatability, decidability, isolation, observability, and maintenance value.

rules: Require at least two linked occurrences before proposing durable promotion.
Prefer an executable harness when the decision is deterministic, then a skill, then wiki knowledge.
Keep proposals pending until a human accepts them; never auto-promote shared policy.

failure-corpus-discipline.label: Failure corpus discipline
failure-corpus-discipline.description: Keep failure learning at the event level so the corpus stays useful instead of filling with duplicate rows.
rules: When recording verification failures, model one logical failure event as one Failure Case; do not split one scope-violation run into one case per changed file.
Do not include volatile outcomes such as actual exit code, timestamps, or workspace fingerprint in the stable failure identity unless they change the root cause.
In the Harness & Skill dashboard, group failures by the human-actionable event before showing counts, and use occurrences for repetition instead of duplicating rows.
Before closing any implemented task, audit every failure discovered during diagnosis, implementation, integration, and live verification even when the final verification passes.
For each discovered failure, record one disposition: link an existing skill or harness, create a narrowly reusable artifact, or explicitly mark it one-off with evidence; never silently omit it.
Prefer strengthening an existing skill or harness over creating a near-duplicate, and include the learning disposition in the final result summary.

task-supersession-lifecycle.label: Task supersession lifecycle
task-supersession-lifecycle.description: Prevent reissued replacement tasks from leaving the original work active on the board or milestone.
rules: When reissuing a task because its scope or contract changed, set supersedesTaskId instead of relying on title suffixes such as R or words such as reissue.
Treat the replacement as the only executable task; when it reaches DONE, archive the original automatically and record supersededByTaskId for auditability.
Before reporting a milestone or board as correct, check for active tasks that have an archived or completed replacement with the same work contract.

runtime-boundary-consistency.label: Runtime boundary consistency
runtime-boundary-consistency.description: Keep browser modules, server authorization, and the actually running process on the same verified revision.
rules: Browser entry modules may import only files reachable from the server static root; verify every relative import over HTTP, not only its source file with node --check.
When server behavior changes, restart the real server process before UI verification; a refreshed browser with an old Node process is a mixed-version failure.
For permissions exposed as UI buttons, test both visibility and the matching API authorization using the same actor role.
After starting a server for a workspace path containing spaces, read bootstrap.workspace.root and compare it to the intended absolute path before running any verification.

delivery-before-done.label: Delivery before DONE
delivery-before-done.description: Prevent reviewed work from being marked complete before its verified worktree output reaches the main branch.
rules: Keep a reviewed task in REVIEW until its worktree changes are committed and merged successfully; approval intent alone is not delivery.
If commit or merge fails, record TASK_MERGE_FAILED, preserve the worktree, and leave the task reviewable instead of marking it DONE.
Block archive while a task worktree contains unlanded changes, and remove completed worktrees only after confirming the files exist on the main branch.
